content.json:
# JA4 / JA4S Threat-Hunting Workbook

Surfaces the most interesting TLS-fingerprint activity in this workspace and explains **why** each item is interesting. Runs on Defender data streamed here (`DeviceNetworkEvents` SslConnectionInspected, `SecurityIncident`/`SecurityAlert`, `ConnectionSuccess` process data).

**How items are scored**
- **Rarity** = combined: rare only if on **few hosts AND few connections**. The **Min rarity** selector focuses every section on the rare tail.
- **Incident-match fidelity** = proximity (flow-exact > host+time > host-any > shared-IP) x temporal decay x rarity x incident severity.
- **Malice signals** = JA4 structure (no-SNI / no-ALPN / legacy TLS / no-extensions / abnormal cipher count), process attribution (LOLBIN / user-path), cert anomalies (self-signed / self-CA / IP-SNI). An age discount demotes aged, widespread fingerprints.
- **Beaconing** = low-jitter periodic call-homes. **First-seen** = brand-new JA4 in the estate. **Cipher-cycling** = one JA4_ac with many cipher variants (evasion).

Pick a **Lookback** and **Min rarity** below, then choose a **Section**. Only the selected section runs (faster). Start at **Top Leads**.

## Top Prioritized Leads - what's interesting and why
The highest-signal items across all engines, ranked. **Why** explains each: a suspicious fingerprint, a fingerprint tied to an incident's device/IP, or a periodic call-home. Work the top (red) rows first.

## Summary
At-a-glance counts. **Rare pairs** and **self-signed certs** are where to start. Use the **Section** selector above to dive in.

## Suspicious rare JA4 - triage
Every rare JA4 x JA4S pair scored for maliciousness. **Verdict / Reasons** say *why* (LOLBIN process, self-signed/self-CA cert, no-SNI, no-extensions, abnormal cipher count...). **Procs** is the process behind the fingerprint (recovered via ConnectionSuccess). Verdict tops out at **Critical**.

## Incident-match fidelity
Which rare fingerprints are tied to your incidents and how tightly. **MatchTier** = flow-exact > host+time > host-any > shared-IP; **MatchFidelity** decays by time-gap and is weighted by rarity and incident severity. **DrivingIncident** is the incident it matched.

## Beaconing call-homes
Regular, low-jitter TLS to one destination = classic C2 shape. A **rare** JA4 to a **no-SNI / raw-IP** destination with low **JitterCV** is the top lead. Benign telemetry (Microsoft, AV, browsers) also beacons - triage by **Dest**.

## New & cipher-cycling fingerprints
**First-seen**: a JA4 absent from the baseline that just appeared - especially one **spreading to multiple hosts** - is a strong new-implant signal. **Cipher-cycling**: many full-JA4 variants sharing one **JA4_ac** = a tool rotating ciphers to evade hashing (GreyNoise pattern).

content.query:
// Workbook: FIRST-SEEN JA4 in environment (brand-new fingerprints in the recent window).
// A JA4 absent from the whole baseline then appearing in the last `newWindow` is a strong new-implant
// signal - especially if it spreads to multiple hosts. (FoxIO: "a sudden new JA4 in a static estate
// is worth investigating".) Anti-join recent-vs-baseline; no rarity gate (new == inherently rare).
let lookback = {Lookback};
let newWindow = 2d;            // "recent" = last 2 days; everything before is the baseline
let base =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "SslConnectionInspected"
    | where AdditionalFields has "ja4"
    | extend AF = todynamic(AdditionalFields)
    | extend ja4_ = tostring(AF.ja4), sni = tostring(AF.server_name)
    | where isnotempty(ja4_)
    | project TimeGenerated, DeviceId, RemoteIP, ja4_, sni;
let historic = materialize(base | where TimeGenerated < ago(newWindow) | distinct ja4_);
base
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(newWindow)
| summarize NewConns = count(), NewDevices = dcount(DeviceId, 4), Dests = make_set(coalesce(sni, tostring(RemoteIP)), 5),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated) by ja4_
| join kind=leftanti historic on ja4_      // present now, NOT in the baseline = brand new
| where NewConns >= 3                       // drop one-off noise
| extend Spread = iff(NewDevices >= 2, "spreading (multi-host)", "single host")
| extend cipherCnt = toint(substring(ja4_, 4, 2)), extCnt = toint(substring(ja4_, 6, 2))
| extend Note = strcat(iff(substring(ja4_, 1, 2) in ("10","11","s3","s2"), "legacy-TLS ", ""),
                       iff(substring(ja4_, 3, 1) == "i", "no-SNI ", ""), iff(extCnt == 0, "no-ext ", ""),
                       iff(cipherCnt between (1 .. 3), "few-ciphers ", ""))
| project ja4_, NewDevices, NewConns, Spread, Note, Dests, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| sort by NewDevices desc, NewConns desc

content.json:
## C2 tradecraft - process vs fingerprint contradictions
**Process -> JA4 mismatch**: the JA4 b-section (chars 10-21) is a stable TLS-library identifier; a browser library from a non-browser process, Python/Go from a LOLBIN, or any TLS from a script-host LOLBIN is high-signal (uTLS parroting / DLL injection / loaders). Microsoft destinations are excluded.

### C2 TLS-shape (Cobalt Strike / Meterpreter)
TLS 1.2 + no-ALPN from a LOLBIN to a **non-Microsoft** destination, or the exact Cobalt Strike client c-section (16bbda4055b2) / server JA4S (t120300_c030_52d195ce1d92).

content.query:
// Workbook: C2 TLS-SHAPE (Cobalt Strike / Meterpreter). Malleable-C2 cannot change the WinINET/WinHTTP
// ClientHello: TLS 1.2 + no-ALPN + a LOLBIN/loader process to a public, non-Microsoft dest. Exact CS tells
// (client c-section 16bbda4055b2 / server JA4S t120300_c030_52d195ce1d92) = CRITICAL on any process.
let lookback = {Lookback};
let csJa4s = "t120300_c030_52d195ce1d92";
let csCsec = "16bbda4055b2";
let suspProcs = dynamic(["rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","msbuild.exe","hh.exe","installutil.exe","regsvcs.exe","regasm.exe"]);
let msSni = dynamic([".microsoft.com",".windows.com",".windowsupdate.com",".office.com",".office365.com",".sharepoint.com",".onedrive.com",".microsoftonline.com",".azure.com",".azureedge.net",".msedge.net",".msftconnecttest.com",".windows.net"]);
let ssl =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "SslConnectionInspected"
    | where AdditionalFields has "ja4"
    | extend AF = todynamic(AdditionalFields)
    | extend ja4_ = tostring(AF.ja4), ja4s_ = tostring(AF.ja4s), sni = tostring(AF.server_name)
    | where strlen(ja4_) >= 36
    | extend tlsVer = substring(ja4_, 1, 2), alpn = substring(ja4_, 8, 2), csec = substring(ja4_, 24, 12)
    | extend isPublic = (isnotempty(RemoteIP) and ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP) == false)
    | where isPublic and ((tlsVer == "12" and alpn == "00") or ja4s_ == csJa4s or csec == csCsec)
    | summarize Conns = count(), DeviceName = take_any(DeviceName), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ja4_, ja4s_, tlsVer, alpn, csec, sni;
let procs =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" and isnotempty(InitiatingProcessFileName)
    | summarize Proc = take_any(tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName)), ProcFolder = take_any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort;
ssl
| join hint.shufflekey=DeviceId kind=inner procs on DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| extend exactCs   = (ja4s_ == csJa4s or csec == csCsec)
| extend shape     = (tlsVer == "12" and alpn == "00")
| extend suspProc  = (Proc in~ (suspProcs))
| extend isUserPath = (ProcFolder has "appdata" or ProcFolder hasprefix "c:\\users\\" or ProcFolder has "\\temp\\")
| extend isMsDest  = (isnotempty(sni) and sni has_any (msSni))
| where exactCs or (shape and suspProc and not(isMsDest))
| extend Why = strcat(iff(csec == csCsec, "CS client c-section; ", ""), iff(ja4s_ == csJa4s, "CS server JA4S; ", ""),
                      iff(shape, "TLS1.2+no-ALPN; ", ""), iff(suspProc, strcat("proc ", Proc, "; "), ""), iff(isUserPath, "user/temp path; ", ""))
| summarize Conns = sum(Conns), Devices = dcount(DeviceId, 4), Procs = make_set(Proc, 5), Dests = make_set(coalesce(sni, tostring(RemoteIP)), 6),
            AnyExactCs = max(exactCs), AnyUserPath = max(isUserPath), Why = take_any(Why), FirstSeen = min(FirstSeen), LastSeen = max(LastSeen)
        by ja4_, ja4s_
| extend Verdict = iff(AnyExactCs, "CRITICAL - Cobalt Strike fingerprint", "HIGH - C2 TLS shape + suspicious process")
| project Verdict, ja4_, ja4s_, Why, Procs, Devices, Conns, AnyUserPath, Dests, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| sort by Verdict asc, Devices asc, Conns asc

content.json:
## JA4 rarity landscape
Fingerprints by **combined rarity** (0 = ubiquitous, 1 = singleton on both hosts and connections). The **right-hand bars are the rare tail** - your hunting ground; the **Min rarity** selector focuses every other section there.

## Known-bad JA4+JA4S lookup (opt-in)
This is the **only** component that uses external reference data: FoxIO's **public** ja4plus-mapping (Sliver, IcedID, Cobalt Strike, SoftEther) - a free public reference embedded as a static table, **not** a premium feed. An exact JA4+JA4S pair hit is **CRITICAL** on its own. Set **Known-bad lookup = On** above to run it. Verify hits before acting; fingerprints rotate ~yearly.

### Methodology & sources
MISP decaying models (temporal decay) | Pyramid of Pain (JA4 ranks above IP) | JA4 / JA4+ spec (structure decode, JA4_ac) | FoxIO ja4plus-mapping (opt-in known-bad only) | detection-engineering proximity scoring | Recorded Future / STIX confidence bands. Core signals are Microsoft E5 / Defender-native - no premium external feeds.
